title: Hieracium phrixoclonum
intro: Hieracium phrixoclonum, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments, including arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium phrixoclonum include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Hawkweed is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is sometimes used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
